stories.headline: Pakistan Lockdown: Pakistan Enforces Lockdown Ahead of US-Iran Talks in Islamabad
stories.summary: Pakistan has imposed a strict lockdown in Islamabad ahead of crucial US-Iran talks scheduled to start on April 11, 2026. The lockdown enhances security and seals off key areas around the Serena Hotel, where the talks will take place. Authorities have imposed sweeping restrictions, including traffic curbs, school closures, and market shutdowns, to ensure the safety of the delegations. The capital's streets are under strict lockdown, with check posts set up and roads shut down. The lockdown is part of Pakistan's efforts to host successful and secure negotiations between the US and Iran, which aim to bring an end to the conflict between the two countries. The talks are considered historic and consequential, and Pakistan is taking all necessary measures to ensure their success.

stories.headline: 650 Iranian missiles fired, 24 killed, and 10,800 Israeli strikes: The war in numbers
stories.summary: Iran has fired 650 missiles at Israel, resulting in the deaths of 20 Israeli civilians and foreign nationals, as well as 4 Palestinians in the West Bank. Over 7,000 people in Israel were injured, according to the Health Ministry. In response, Israel has launched 10,800 strikes. The conflict began after the US and Israel attacked Iran on February 28, targeting nuclear, military, and civilian sites. Iran has retaliated by launching attacks on US troops in the Middle East and Israeli military facilities. The Israeli Air Force reports that 92% of the 400 missiles fired by Iran were intercepted. The conflict has escalated into a full-scale war, with the US and Israel intensifying their attacks on Iran, including bombing the country's top university. At least 24 people have been killed in the conflict.

stories.headline: Ocean temperatures near record high in March: EU monitor
stories.summary: Ocean temperatures reached near record highs in March, according to the European Union's Copernicus monitor. The average sea surface temperature was 20.97 °C, the second-highest value ever recorded for the month. This temperature is only surpassed by the record set in March 2024 during the last El Niño event. Globally, March was the fourth-warmest on record, with temperatures 1.48 °C above pre-industrial levels. The high sea surface temperatures were recorded over the extra-polar oceans, spanning 60 degrees south to 60 degrees north. This data indicates that the world's oceans are continuing to warm, with significant implications for global climate patterns. The Copernicus monitor tracks sea surface temperatures as part of its climate change monitoring efforts, providing valuable insights into the state of the planet's oceans.

stories.headline: British sentiment toward Trump’s US plunges amid Iran war
stories.summary: More than half of Brits view the US as a negative force in the world, according to recent polling. A survey found 65% of British adults believe America tends to create problems for other countries, up from 46% in December. Conversely, only 22% think the US helps solve problems, down from 35% late last year. The British public is divided on supporting the US in protecting shipping and reopening the Strait of Hormuz, with 42% in favor and 36% opposed in one poll, although another survey showed opposing results. The shift in sentiment comes amid the escalating Iran war and tensions between US President Donald Trump and British Prime Minister Keir Starmer, indicating a fracturing of the once robust UK-US relationship. British voters' trust in America has plummeted, reflecting a significant decline in positive perceptions of the US.

stories.headline: WhatsApp Introduces Username Feature for Connecting Without Sharing Phone Numbers WhatsApp is preparing to roll out a long-anticipated username feature that will allow users to communicate without ever revealing their phone numbers, a signifi
stories.summary: WhatsApp is rolling out a username feature that allows users to communicate without sharing their phone numbers. This update will provide stronger privacy controls and change how people connect on the platform. The feature has been long-anticipated and fulfills a user wish to use the app without revealing their phone number. With usernames, users can connect and chat without sharing their phone numbers, making communication simpler and more secure. The rollout is part of Meta's efforts to enhance user privacy and security on WhatsApp. The update is expected to make it easier for users to connect with others without compromising their personal information. This new feature will provide an additional layer of privacy and security for WhatsApp users.

stories.headline: Google Meet Rolls Out Speech Translation to Mobile Version-Here's How to Use It Google Meet on mobile now has a feature that offers a way to translate speech, so you can better understand who you're talking to.
stories.summary: Google Meet has rolled out a speech translation feature to its mobile version, allowing users to better understand conversations in different languages. The feature, which was previously available on the web, translates speech into the user's preferred language in real-time. To access the feature, users can go to the Settings, then the Tools section, and enable "Speech Translation". The feature currently supports translation between English and five other languages: Spanish, French, German, Portuguese, and Italian. It is available on Android and iOS devices for subscribers with select Google AI and Workspace subscriptions. This update enables mobile users to participate in multilingual meetings seamlessly, making it a useful feature for those who attend meetings on the go. The rollout began recently, following the initial launch on the web in January.

stories.headline: DJI to launch Osmo Pocket 4 action camera series next week with 1 DJI said on Thursday it will launch its highly anticipated Osmo Pocket 4 series action cameras on April 16.
stories.summary: DJI will launch its Osmo Pocket 4 series action cameras on April 16. The launch event, titled "The World In My Pocket", is scheduled for 12 PM GMT (8 AM ET / 5 AM PT). The standard model of the Osmo Pocket 4 features a 1-inch sensor, offering 14 stops of dynamic range and improved capabilities. DJI confirmed the launch through a teaser post on its X account on April 9, with the tagline "The World In My Pocket". The company has been building anticipation for the release, and the official unveiling will take place on April 16. The Osmo Pocket 4 series is expected to retain the line's signature design, with further details to be revealed at the launch event. The launch will be a significant update to DJI's action camera lineup.

stories.headline: This developer squeezed a full Minecraft server onto an ESP32, and it actually works Barely, but it works.
stories.summary: A developer, SoPortalRunner, has successfully created a custom Minecraft server from scratch in C, running on an ESP32 microcontroller. The server, called bareiron, is a stripped-down version of the game, allowing it to operate on the limited hardware. The ESP32, typically used for IoT projects, has minimal resources compared to standard Minecraft server requirements, which usually need gigabytes of RAM and a gigahertz-range processor. Despite the constraints, PortalRunner's implementation works, albeit slowly. This achievement demonstrates the developer's ability to optimize performance and work closely with the hardware. The project showcases the potential for running complex applications on resource-constrained devices, with the ESP32 Minecraft server being the smallest and cheapest to date. The custom server was written from the ground up, allowing for efficient use of the ESP32's limited resources.

stories.headline: Sabres shut out Blue Jackets Colten Ellis shines, Sabres win 5-0
stories.summary: The Buffalo Sabres defeated the Columbus Blue Jackets 5-0 at KeyBank Center on Thursday. Colten Ellis made 37 saves to record the first shutout of his NHL career. Josh Doan scored twice in the win. The Blue Jackets' record now stands at 39-28-12 with 90 points, leaving them four points out of a wild-card spot and two points out of third in the Metro division. Ellis's shutout performance denied the Blue Jackets any scoring opportunities, as they were unable to get a single puck past him. The win marks a significant milestone for Ellis, who earned his first career shutout in the Sabres' dominant 5-0 victory.

stories.headline: Boston Celtics vs. New York Knicks Live Score and Stats
stories.summary: The New York Knicks defeated the Boston Celtics 111-89. Josh Hart led the Knicks to victory with a game-high 26 points, making 5-7 three-pointers. The game was played on February 8, 2026. Jayson Tatum and Payton Pritchard were notable players for the Celtics. The Knicks' win was fueled by Hart's strong performance, which helped secure the 22-point margin of victory. The final score and game recap are available, including player box scores, video, and shot charts. The game was part of the National Basketball Association schedule, with the Knicks and Celtics competing in the Eastern Conference. The win was a significant one for the Knicks, with Hart's 26 points being a key factor in the outcome.

stories.headline: Philadelphia Flyers vs. Detroit Red Wings NHL Game recap for the Philadelphia Philadelphia Flyers vs. Detroit Detroit Red Wings game on April 9, 2026
stories.summary: The Detroit Red Wings defeated the Philadelphia Flyers 6-3 on April 9, 2026. Dylan Larkin led the Red Wings with a hat trick, and the team scored four times on the power play. Patrick Kane contributed a goal and two assists to the win. The Red Wings' victory was sealed in the third period when Kane scored, followed by Larkin's completion of his hat trick. The game was played at Little Caesars Arena, with the Red Wings ultimately emerging victorious. The win gives the Red Wings hope for a playoff spot. The final score was 6-3 in favor of the Red Wings, with Larkin's hat trick being a key factor in the team's success. The game's highlights are available to watch, showcasing the key moments from the matchup.

stories.headline: Toronto Maple Leafs vs. New York Islanders NHL Game recap for the Toronto Toronto Maple Leafs vs. New York New York Islanders game on April 9, 2026
stories.summary: The New York Islanders defeated the Toronto Maple Leafs 5-3 at UBS Arena on April 9, 2026. The win marked the debut victory for new Islanders coach Peter DeBoer. The game was part of the ongoing NHL season, with the Islanders securing the win despite the Maple Leafs' efforts. The final score saw the Islanders emerge on top, with a two-goal margin. The game's stats, highlights, and play-by-play are available for review, showcasing the performance of both teams. The matchup was closely followed, with fans able to track every play throughout the game on various sports platforms, including CBS Sports and ESPN. The win was a significant start for DeBoer's tenure as coach of the Islanders.

stories.headline: MahaRERA: ‘Paper allotment of parking slot not enough’; real estate developer must provide usable space
stories.summary: The Maharashtra Real Estate Regulatory Authority (MahaRERA) has directed a real estate developer to allot an alternate parking space to a homebuyer until the promised parking space is constructed. MahaRERA stated that mere paper allotment of a parking slot in a building under construction is not sufficient, and the developer must provide a usable covered car parking space once consideration for it has been collected. The authority emphasized that a valid parking allotment must be physically usable and accessible. The developer was instructed to provide an alternative parking space within a defined timeframe until the originally promised facility is operational. This directive establishes that developers must provide usable and accessible vehicle bays to residents, and paper-based parking allotments for structures under development do not satisfy contractual obligations.

stories.headline: India and Pakistan: Neighbours Who Do Not Talk to Each Other
stories.summary: India and Pakistan, two neighbouring countries with a history of tension, have not been engaging in direct talks. Despite launching confidence-building measures such as high-level talks and easing visa restrictions, the peace process was recently dismissed by the Pakistani government. A bilateral ceasefire was negotiated between the two countries, but ceasefire violations have occurred. The US has attempted to facilitate talks, with Secretary of State Marco Rubio announcing plans for discussions at a neutral site. However, Indian officials have not yet expressed readiness for talks. Pakistan has also sought Chinese backing for a five-point peace plan, while India's strategic community is divided on how to approach the situation, with some arguing that India should have explored a mediatory role to avoid appearing absent in the region.

stories.headline: ‘Tamil Nadu won’t bow down’: CM Stalin
stories.summary: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister M K Stalin stated that the state won't bow down, emphasizing no compromise on state rights or federalism. His remarks came in response to Prime Minister Modi's comments at an NDA rally, where Modi accused the DMK government of corruption and predicted its defeat in the 2026 Assembly elections. Modi also criticized the state's handling of drugs and crime. Stalin countered, saying "Tamil Nadu will not bow to Delhi's arrogance." Additionally, Stalin criticized the Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Amendment Bill, 2026, arguing it targets minority communities, particularly Christian institutions. Stalin expressed confidence that the DMK's welfare schemes and ideology will secure a mandate for the Dravidian Model 2.0, despite Modi's predictions of the party's defeat. The exchange reflects ongoing tensions between the state government and the central government ahead of the 2026 Assembly elections.

stories.headline: Declaration of Holiday on 14th April, 2026 - Birthday of Dr. B.R. Ambedkar: DoP&T Order dated 09.04.2026
stories.summary: The Government of India has declared April 14, 2026, as a closed holiday for all Central Government Offices and industrial establishments. This is to mark the 135th birth anniversary of Dr. B.R. Ambedkar, a social reformer and principal architect of the Indian Constitution. The Department of Personnel & Training issued an order on April 9, 2026, announcing the public holiday. Ambedkar Jayanti, also known as Bhim Jayanti, is celebrated annually on April 14 to honor Dr. Ambedkar's contributions. In 2026, the day falls on a Tuesday, and schools, colleges, banks, and offices across various states will observe closures to commemorate the occasion. The holiday is a tribute to Dr. Ambedkar's legacy and allows citizens to pay their respects and participate in celebrations across the country.

stories.headline: Gift Nifty signals gap-up opening as global cues and geopolitics drive market outlook Gift Nifty points to a gap-up start as global markets rise, but FII outflows, crude oil moves and Iran-US tensions keep sentiment cautious.
stories.summary: Gift Nifty signals a gap-up opening, driven by positive global cues, despite cautious sentiment due to ongoing geopolitical uncertainty in the Iran-US corridor. Foreign Institutional Investor (FII) outflows and crude oil price movements are also weighing on market sentiment. The transfer of derivative contracts to Gift Nifty is expected to bring a bigger liquidity pool to Indian markets. Crude oil remains a significant macro risk amid Middle East tensions, while continued foreign selling is restricting sharp upside. Gift Nifty's gap-up start may be sustained if follow-through buying occurs. The market is closely watching global cues, FII activity, and crude oil prices, which will determine the sustainability of the gap-up opening. Overall, investors remain cautious, and market sentiment is mixed, with global tensions and macroeconomic risks influencing the outlook.

stories.headline: Global Investors Recalibrate Strategies Amid Geopolitical Tensions And Emerging Market Shifts New data shows investors aren't merely reacting to market volatility; they're redesigning long‑term capital allocation strategies to balance geopolitics, indust
stories.summary: Global investors are recalibrating their long-term capital allocation strategies to balance geopolitics, industrial policy, and growth opportunities. Amidst market volatility, investors are hedging against uncertainty, rotating sectors, and prioritizing resilience over growth. The stabilization of global geopolitical risk indices in Q4 2025 has allowed investors to pivot from defensive assets. However, the recent conflict in the Middle East has driven up energy prices, putting inflation hedging back on the agenda. Investors are advised to set a system, define target allocation, and rebalance systematically during crises, rather than reacting to the latest news. This approach helps investors navigate the new era of significant trade and capital flow shifts driven by modern mercantilism, and prepares them for the broad impact of inflation on their portfolios. Investors are redesigning their strategies to adapt to the changing landscape.

stories.headline: No other headlines were provided to select from.
stories.summary: Personal income in the US fell in February, while inflation remained steady at 3%. The latest economic data indicates a decline in personal income, with inflation holding at 3% for the month. In other news, the 2026 Masters golf tournament has begun, with honorary starters Jack Nicklaus, Gary Player, and Tom Watson teeing off. Meanwhile, news outlets such as The Washington Post, NPR, and the New York Post continue to provide live coverage and updates on breaking stories, both nationally and internationally. Reuters.com also offers comprehensive coverage of international news, with the latest headlines and updates from around the globe. These news sources provide in-depth coverage of major events, including politics, business, science, and technology.

stories.headline: No other headlines were provided to select from.
stories.summary: The daughter of a high-ranking Iranian regime official is facing deportation from the United States. She was previously forced to leave Emory University due to backlash. The controversy surrounding her attendance at the university led to a public outcry, resulting in her departure. The incident has garnered significant attention, with various news outlets covering the story, including CNN, Bloomberg, and NBC News. The situation highlights the complexities and sensitivities surrounding individuals with ties to authoritarian regimes attending educational institutions in the US. The daughter's impending deportation is the latest development in the ongoing saga, which has sparked widespread discussion and debate.

stories.headline: Why stock market is rising today? Sensex surges 500 pts, Nifty above 23,900. 3 key reasons behind Rs 4 lakh crore gains Indian stock markets surged on Friday, with Sensex and Nifty climbing approximately 0.7%.
stories.summary: Indian stock markets surged on Friday, with Sensex jumping 525.11 points to 85,006.92 and Nifty50 climbing 147.50 points to 25,963.30. The markets gained approximately 0.7%, driven by hopes of an Iran-US ceasefire and falling oil prices. Additionally, US President Donald Trump's announcement to pause 26% reciprocal tariffs on Indian exports for 90 days boosted investor sentiment. Despite this, foreign investors remained net sellers. The rally was also supported by global market gains, declining crude oil prices, and renewed FII buying. Analysts expect a positive economic outlook to provide a durable tailwind for equities, with the market's future trend depending on the outcome of upcoming peace talks. The surge resulted in Rs 4 lakh crore gains, with Sensex and Nifty logging gains of over 1,100 points and 385 points respectively in early trade.

stories.headline: Sensex Jumps 919 Points as Financial Stocks Lead Market Rebound Indian benchmark indices Sensex and Nifty surged over 1 per cent, with the Sensex jumping 918.60 points, driven by strong buying in banking and financial counte
stories.summary: Indian benchmark indices Sensex and Nifty surged over 1% on Friday, with the Sensex jumping 918.60 points to 77,550.25 and the Nifty rising 275.50 points to 24,050.60. The market rebound was driven by strong buying in banking and financial counters, as well as a positive trend in global equities. Financial stocks, which were heavily sold by foreign portfolio investors in March, led the gains. The sector had seen outflows exceeding ₹60,000 crore, the highest since 2012. The rally was broad-based, with the Bank Nifty also posting significant gains. The surge in the market came after a period of decline, with the Sensex and Nifty recovering from recent losses. The positive trend in global equities also contributed to the market's upward momentum.

stories.headline: Stock Market LIVE: Sensex, Nifty 50 poised to open higher on TCS earnings boost Latest news and live updates from the stock market today, as well as Sensex and Nifty 50, rupee-dollar exchange rate, and latest business news from India Inc.|
stories.summary: Indian stocks ended higher on Thursday, with the Sensex and Nifty recording 52-week highs, nearing their all-time highs. The market was buoyed by optimism around an earnings revival and renewed foreign inflows. Investor sentiment was lifted by expectations of strong festive-season demand, recent tax cuts, and policy support. The Sensex and Nifty 50 are poised to open higher today, driven by a boost from TCS earnings. The rupee-dollar exchange rate and other currencies are also being closely watched. The stock market is expected to continue its upward trend, with investors tracking real-time insights on Sensex, Nifty, and other benchmark indices. The latest business news from India Inc. and global indices such as DAX, Hang Seng, and Nasdaq are also being monitored for their impact on the Indian stock market.

stories.headline: Stock Market Today LIVE: Gift Nifty signals positive start for Nifty 50, Sensex; TCS, Wipro, JSW Steel in focus Stock Market Today LIVE: Asian markets traded higher, while the US stock market ended higher overnight, amid hopes of peace talks between the US and Iran.
stories.summary: Asian markets traded higher and the US stock market ended higher overnight, amid hopes of peace talks between the US and Iran. Indian markets are set for a firm start today, buoyed by strong domestic cues and GIFT Nifty trading higher. The GIFT Nifty on the NSE IX traded higher by 288.50 points, or 1.25 per cent, at 23,311, signaling a positive start for the Nifty 50 and Sensex. Sectorally, Nifty Auto led the gainers with a sharp 2.8% increase, while Nifty IT was the only index to end lower. Top gainers included M&M, Tata Motors, and HCL Tech, with broader markets ending at 0.50% gains. Key stocks in focus today include TCS, Wipro, and JSW Steel, with technical indicators suggesting an upward bias despite global headwinds.

stories.headline: Stocks rally on optimism over Iran war ceasefire, oil extends gains
stories.summary: Stocks rose on Friday as investors remained optimistic about the ceasefire in the US-Iran war ahead of planned weekend talks. The two-week truce, announced on Tuesday, aims to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, which has been closed since the start of the war. However, oil prices extended gains due to concerns that Israel's continued attacks on Lebanon could disrupt the peace process. Additionally, Saudi Arabia's warning about its production capacity, which has been cut due to Iran's attacks on its infrastructure, contributed to nervousness among oil traders. The truce and potential reopening of the Strait of Hormuz had initially sparked optimism, but ongoing tensions and concerns about oil production capacity are keeping oil prices elevated. The situation remains volatile, with investors and oil traders closely watching developments ahead of the weekend talks.

stories.headline: EM Stocks Set for Best Weekly Gain Since 2020 Before Iran Talks
stories.summary: Emerging-market stocks are on track for their biggest weekly gain since 2020, driven by hopes of easing Middle East tensions ahead of US-Iran talks. Investors are optimistic about a potential de-escalation of the conflict, which has led to a broad-based rally in emerging-market assets across Asia and Latin America. The surge follows a US-Iran ceasefire, which caused oil prices to tumble, reducing volatility in emerging markets. The gain is the largest in close to six years, with emerging-market assets initially posting modest gains amid speculation that US President Donald Trump may back down from escalating the war in Iran. The rally highlights the concentration of emerging market funds in Asian economies and their sensitivity to geopolitical developments, particularly those affecting oil prices.

stories.headline: Long queues at EV chargers highlight gaps in NSW infrastructure
stories.summary: Long queues at electric vehicle (EV) chargers in regional New South Wales over the Easter long weekend highlighted significant gaps in charging infrastructure. The majority of EV chargers are located in eastern New South Wales, leaving regional areas with limited access. Drivers faced queues of up to five hours at charging stations, exposing major gaps in infrastructure nationwide. The recent surge in EV sales in Australia has led to a 50% increase in demand, outpacing the development of charging infrastructure. This has raised concerns about the adequacy of current resources, particularly in regional areas. The shortage of charging stations may lead to longer wait times, with queues of up to six hours possible, underscoring the need for urgent investment in EV charging infrastructure across Australia.

stories.headline: Blackout persists as Adelabu’s 6,000MW target misses three deadlines
stories.summary: Nigeria has missed its 6,000MW power generation target for the third time under Minister Adelabu. The target was initially expected to be achieved, but vandalism affecting critical power transmission equipment and grid disturbances prevented the system from reaching the goal. Minister Adelabu acknowledged the failure, citing the actions of vandals as a major setback. Despite initial confidence in achieving the target, Adelabu later acknowledged constraints that could affect delivery. The failure to meet the target has resulted in persistent blackouts, with gas shortages and grid failures contributing to the nation's power woes. Adelabu had previously briefed on the challenges, explaining that grid disturbances prevented the system from reaching 6,000MW by the end of 2024. The missed target has significant implications for Nigeria's power sector and the country's overall development.
